Homework 6 - House Prices

Dibya Gyawali

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
load("/Users/dibyagyawali/Desktop/Data Visualization Project/house_prices.rda")

house_prices <- house_prices %>%
  mutate(year = as.numeric(format(date, "%Y")))

2(a) House Price Index by State

ggplot(house_prices, aes(x = year, y = house_price_index)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ state, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1980, 2000, 2020), 
                     labels = c("'80", "'00", "'20")) +
  labs(title = "House Price Index Trends by State",
       x = "Year", y = "House Price Index") +
  theme_minimal()

2(b) Reshape Data

house_reshaped <- house_prices %>%
  gather(key = "measure", value = "value", 
         house_price_index, unemploy_perc)
head(house_reshaped)
## # A tibble: 6 × 6
##   date       state   house_price_perc  year measure           value
##   <date>     <chr>              <dbl> <dbl> <chr>             <dbl>
## 1 1976-01-01 Alabama           0.0404  1976 house_price_index  37.1
## 2 1976-02-01 Alabama           0.0518  1976 house_price_index  37.8
## 3 1976-03-01 Alabama           0.0593  1976 house_price_index  38.3
## 4 1976-04-01 Alabama           0.0596  1976 house_price_index  38.6
## 5 1976-05-01 Alabama           0.0557  1976 house_price_index  38.7
## 6 1976-06-01 Alabama           0.0536  1976 house_price_index  38.8

2(c) Dual-Line Plot

ggplot(house_reshaped, aes(x = year, y = value, color = measure)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ state, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1980, 2000, 2020), 
                     labels = c("'80", "'00", "'20")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("house_price_index" = "steelblue", 
                                "unemploy_perc" = "coral"),
                     labels = c("House Price Index", "Unemployment %")) +
  labs(title = "House Price Index and Unemployment Rate by State") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

Comment

This graph is not ideal because house_price_index and unemploy_perc are measured on very different scales. Plotting them on the same axis can be misleading and makes it difficult to compare trends accurately. A better approach would be to use separate plots or normalize the values.
